title: 11 Best Herbal Teas For Cavities
intro: Herbal teas for cavities are a blend of plant-based ingredients that have been traditionally used to prevent and treat tooth decay, gum diseases, and other oral health issues. These teas are rich in antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties that help to kill bacteria, reduce plaque, and promote healing in the mouth. Some examples of herbal teas used to treat cavities include sage tea, which has antibacterial properties that help to prevent the growth of oral bacteria; neem tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce gum inflammation; peppermint tea, which freshens breath and reduces plaque; licorice root tea, which has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties; and chamomile tea, which soothes and calms the mouth. Additionally, other herbal teas like eucalyptus, clove, and myrrh have been used to treat cavities due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to prevent tooth decay and gum diseases.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to treat tooth decay and cavities due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a primary cause of tooth decay. Drinking ginger tea helps to reduce the formation of dental plaque, prevent the progression of cavities, and alleviate toothache and gum inflammation. The benefits of using ginger tea to treat cavities include its natural and non-toxic nature, making it a popular alternative to conventional treatments for oral health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of fresh Zingiber officinale (ginger) root.
Wash the ginger root with water, then peel it with a knife.
Cut the peeled ginger into small pieces and put them in a cup.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to the cup with ginger pieces, then let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and drink it while warm, 2-3 times a day to help prevent cavities.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can help alleviate symptoms of cavities, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness due to the high concentration of gingerols and shogaols. Precautions to take include avoiding consumption before bedtime, as it may interfere with sleep, and reducing the frequency of consumption if experiencing digestive issues, such as diarrhea or bloating.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2 times a day.
Take 2 tablespoons of Zingiber officinale powder 30 minutes before meals.
Use Zingiber officinale tea as mouthwash 2 times a day.
Gargle with Zingiber officinale tea for 30 seconds 2 times a day.
Continue treatment for 2-3 months to see optimal results.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat cavities, a common oral health issue, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, including alkylamides, caffeic acid, and chicoric acid, help to reduce the severity of cavities by inhibiting the growth of oral bacteria and promoting wound healing. The polyphenols in Echinacea purpurea teas also exhibit antioxidant properties, which aid in reducing oxidative stress and preventing the progression of cavities. Regular consumption of Echinacea purpurea teas may help to prevent and treat cavities by reducing the risk of tooth decay and promoting overall oral health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the Echinacea flowers and leaves in a cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it using a fine mesh sieve.
Squeeze out as much liquid as possible from the Echinacea mixture into the cup.
Drink the Echinacea tea warm, 2-3 times a day, for best results.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat cavities, but they may cause side effects such as mild stomach upset, allergic reactions, or interactions with other medications due to their potential effects on blood sugar and blood pressure. To use Echinacea purpurea teas safely, it's essential to take precautions such as starting with small doses, monitoring blood sugar and blood pressure levels, and avoiding consumption if you have any pre-existing conditions like diabetes or hypertension.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea 2 times a day.
Use 2-3 teaspoons of dried Echinacea purpurea roots per cup of water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to release its properties.
Drink the tea 3-4 days before dental procedures or appointments.
Combine Echinacea purpurea tea with regular oral hygiene practices.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas, also known as sage tea, have been traditionally used to treat cavities and oral health issues. The antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce the growth of bacteria and inflammation that can cause tooth decay and cavities. The bioactive constituents, including rosmarinic acid, caffeic acid, and triterpenoids, in Salvia officinalis teas exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities that help to prevent the formation of cavities and promote oral health. Drinking sage tea regularly can help to prevent cavities by reducing the risk of tooth decay and gum disease, promoting a healthy oral environment and overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves or 2 teaspoons of dried leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the Salvia officinalis leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help prevent cavities.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of cancer research and clinical oncology", Salvia officinalis teas, which are derived from a plant that has good evidence for alleviating symptoms of oral mucositis and gingivitis, may be beneficial in preventing or treating cavities.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be effective in treating cavities due to their antimicrobial properties, but they may cause gastrointestinal side effects such as stomach upset and diarrhea in some individuals. To use Salvia officinalis teas safely, it is recommended to consume them in moderation and avoid taking them with other medications that may interact with their active compounds, and also to store them properly to prevent contamination.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup Salvia officinalis tea after breakfast and lunch daily.
Take 5-7 tablespoons of dried Salvia leaves and steep in 1 cup of boiling water.
Mouthwash with 5-7 tablespoons of Salvia leaves and 1 cup of water 2 times a day.
Brush teeth with a toothpaste containing Salvia officinalis extract 2 times a day.
Visit a dentist regularly for cavity check-ups and professional cleaning.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used to treat dental cavities due to their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents, including eucalyptol and flavonoids, help to reduce the growth of bacteria that cause cavities and prevent the formation of acid, thereby preventing tooth decay. By inhibiting the production of bacterial enzymes and reducing inflammation, eucalyptus globulus teas help to treat cavities by preventing the progression of the disease. The benefits of using eucalyptus globulus teas for treating cavities include a reduction in pain, inflammation, and the risk of further complications, making it a natural and effective remedy for maintaining good oral health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Eucalyptus globulus leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Chop the Eucalyptus leaves into smaller pieces and place them in a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the Eucalyptus leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the Eucalyptus leaves.
Add honey to taste, if desired, and drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate cavity symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Antibiotics (Basel, Switzerland)", Eucalyptus globulus teas for cavities may have antimicrobial activity against Streptococcus mutans, a common bacteria associated with tooth decay, as it contains 1,8-Cineole (Eucalyptol) which has been shown to inhibit biofilm formation and reduce the biomass of the bacteria.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can help alleviate cavities symptoms by reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, but it may cause gastrointestinal issues such as nausea and diarrhea due to its high eucalyptol content. To minimize side effects, it's recommended to use Eucalyptus globulus teas in moderation, avoid consuming it on an empty stomach, and not to use it as a substitute for regular oral hygiene practices.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Eucalyptus globulus tea, 3 times a day.
Swish 1 tablespoon of tea in mouth for 2 minutes, 3 times a day.
Hold tea in mouth for 2 minutes before spitting it out.
Repeat steps 2-3 for 2-3 weeks for noticeable cavity improvement.
Consult dentist regularly to monitor cavity progress and adjust treatment.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, derived from the roots of the licorice plant, have been traditionally used to treat cavities and oral health issues. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to treat cavities by reducing inflammation and preventing the growth of bacteria that cause tooth decay. The bioactive constituents, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, in Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a bacterium associated with tooth decay, and promote the healing of oral tissues. Regular consumption of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can help to prevent and treat cavities, promoting overall oral health and well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots. If using fresh, dry them in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2 hours.
Grind the roots into small pieces using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Measure out 1 tablespoon of ground Glycyrrhiza glabra roots and add it to 1 cup of boiling water.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help prevent cavities and promote oral health.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Indian journal of dental research : official publication of Indian Society for Dental Research", Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have potential antibacterial activity against primary plaque colonizers and periodontal pathogens with the highest mean zone of inhibition measuring 9.2 ± 1.09 mm and 10.6 ± 0.54 mm at 24 h, respectively.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat cavities, but possible side effects may include increased blood pressure, edema, and an imbalance of potassium levels in the body, which can lead to muscle weakness and heart problems. To use Glycyrrhiza glabra teas safely for treating cavities, it is recommended to limit the consumption to a maximum of 2-3 cups per day, avoid taking it for extended periods, and monitor blood pressure regularly.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 3 times a day.
Take 1/4 teaspoon of powdered Glycyrrhiza glabra 3 times a day.
Swish 5-7 mL of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea in mouth 2-3 times a day.
Maintain this routine for 2-3 weeks to see improvement.
Combine with regular dental care and fluoride treatments.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas, derived from the rhizomes of the Curcuma longa plant, have been traditionally used to treat cavities and promote oral health. The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of Curcuma longa teas help to prevent the growth of bacteria that cause cavities, thereby reducing the risk of tooth decay. The bioactive constituents, including curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, have been shown to inhibit the production of Streptococcus mutans, a primary cause of tooth decay. By using Curcuma longa teas, individuals can benefit from reduced tooth sensitivity, fresher breath, and a lower risk of developing cavities, making it a promising natural remedy for oral health.
preparation_procedure: Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried Curcuma longa root to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for cavity relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of International Society of Preventive & Community Dentistry", Curcuma longa teas may serve as a promising alternative in managing chronic gingivitis and potentially help in preventing cavities due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be effective in treating cavities, but its use may lead to side effects such as gastrointestinal issues, nausea, and diarrhea, particularly if consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to use Curcuma longa teas in moderation, avoid consuming it on an empty stomach, and monitor its impact on your body to adjust the dosage accordingly.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Curcuma longa tea twice a day.
Swish the tea in your mouth for 30 seconds after drinking.
Spit the tea out and do not swallow it.
Repeat for 7-10 days to see noticeable results.
Consult a dentist for further treatment if needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, also known as thyme tea, have been traditionally used to treat oral health issues, including cavities. The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of thyme tea help to combat the growth of bacteria that cause cavities, while also reducing inflammation and pain associated with the ailment. The bioactive constituents of thyme tea, including thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity, inhibiting the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a primary cause of tooth decay. Regular consumption of thyme tea has been found to promote oral health, prevent the development of cavities, and even help to remineralize teeth, making it a valuable natural remedy for treating and preventing cavities.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh thyme leaves (Thymus vulgaris) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the thyme leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the thyme leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the thyme tea to sweeten it.
Drink 1/2 cup of the thyme tea 2-3 times a day to help prevent cavities.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of infection in developing countries", Thymus vulgaris teas for cavities may have antimicrobial activity against Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacterial strains.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can be used to treat cavities, but it may cause side effects such as mouth dryness, tooth sensitivity, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using Thymus vulgaris teas to treat cavities, including not consuming it in excess, avoiding it during pregnancy or breastfeeding, and monitoring for any signs of adverse reactions.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Thymus vulgaris tea 2 times a day
Soak a cotton ball in the tea and apply to cavity
Leave the cotton ball on for 10-15 minutes
Repeat application for 7-10 days to see improvement
Consume 1 teaspoon of Thymus vulgaris powder with water daily

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat cavities and oral health issues, thanks to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. preparations.preparation_study: According to "PloS one", Rosmarinus officinalis teas for cavities may be effective against oral infections due to its moderate to high antibiofilm activity comparable to that of chlorhexidine against S. mutans, with MIC values ranging from 0.08 to 5.00 mg ml-1.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be used to treat cavities ailment, but its long-term consumption may cause side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, or interactions with medications, which can worsen the condition. To minimize potential risks, individuals should be cautious when using Rosmarinus officinalis teas to treat cavities ailment, especially those with pre-existing kidney problems, as it may exacerbate kidney function, and also avoid consuming it during pregnancy and breastfeeding.

Add 1 cup of boiling water to the cup and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.

preparations.herb_desc: Artemisia absinthium teas have been traditionally used to treat cavities and related oral health issues due to their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The herbal preparation's bioactive constituents, including thujone, camphor, and borneol, help to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause cavities, such as Streptococcus mutans, while also reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy oral environment. By reducing the levels of bacteria and inflammation, Artemisia absinthium teas help to prevent the progression of cavities and promote the healing of affected areas. The benefits of using Artemisia absinthium teas to treat cavities include a reduced risk of tooth decay, a decrease in oral pain and discomfort, and a promotion of overall oral health and well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Artemisia absinthium leaves.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the leaves in a cup.
Steep the leaves for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey to the liquid, if desired, to sweeten it.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, after meals, to help prevent cavities.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Iranian journal of basic medical sciences, Artemisia absinthium teas were not mentioned as a treatment for cavities in the provided study, which focused on the use of various plants in Traditional Persian Medicine for gastrointestinal ailments.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Artemisia absinthium teas can be used to treat cavities, but it may cause gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its bitter and astringent properties. Precautions should be taken when using Artemisia absinthium teas, including avoiding excessive consumption, as it may lead to headaches, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat tooth decay and cavities due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the growth of bacteria that cause dental caries. This herbal preparation is believed to help treat cavities by reducing plaque formation, preventing the adhesion of bacteria to tooth surfaces, and promoting a healthy oral microbiome. The bioactive constituents of Lavandula angustifolia teas, including linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to exhibit antibacterial and anti-inflammatory activities, which contribute to their potential in treating cavities. Regular consumption of Lavandula angustifolia teas may help to prevent tooth decay and cavities by reducing the risk of bacterial infections and promoting a healthy oral environment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the oils.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup, discarding the flowers.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, to help soothe the mouth.
Drink the tea 3-4 times a day to help reduce cavity pain and inflammation.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used as a potential treatment for cavities, but it may cause side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, or an increased risk of bleeding due to its anticoagulant properties. To safely use Lavandula angustifolia teas for this purpose, it is recommended to start with a low dose, monitor your body's response, and avoid consuming it if you have bleeding disorders or are taking anticoagulant medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Syzygium aromaticum teas, also known as clove teas, have been used for centuries to treat dental caries and cavities due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Syzygium aromaticum, including eugenol, caryophyllene, and beta-caryophyllene, help to reduce the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a primary cause of tooth decay, and alleviate pain and inflammation associated with cavities. The benefits of using Syzygium aromaticum teas to treat cavities include reduced risk of tooth decay, minimized pain and discomfort, and improved oral health. By incorporating Syzygium aromaticum teas into one's oral hygiene routine, individuals can effectively prevent and treat cavities, promoting a healthier and more radiant smile.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 teaspoon of dried Syzygium aromaticum (clove buds) and grind them into powder.
Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or on the stove.
Add 1/2 teaspoon of the ground Syzygium aromaticum powder to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool down before using it as a mouthwash to prevent cavities.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Syzygium aromaticum teas can be used to treat cavities, but it may cause side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications. To use Syzygium aromaticum teas safely, it's essential to start with small amounts, avoid mixing with other herbs or medications, and discontinue use if any adverse reactions occur.

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas, also known as yarrow tea, have been traditionally used to treat cavities due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents, such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, help to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause tooth decay, while also reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of oral tissues. Drinking yarrow tea may help to treat cavities by reducing the severity of symptoms, preventing further damage, and promoting the remineralization of tooth enamel. The benefits of using Achillea millefolium teas to treat cavities include a reduced risk of tooth decay, fewer dental infections, and a healthier oral microbiome.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Achillea millefolium flowers and 1 cup of water.
Boil the water and pour it over the dried flowers in a cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Take 1/2 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help prevent cavities.
Store any leftover tea in the fridge for up to 3 days.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Medicines (Basel, Switzerland), Achillea millefolium teas for cavities have no specific information mentioned in the provided study, as the study focuses on the review of Cherokee ethnobotanical literature and the published phytochemical investigations on Cherokee medicinal plants.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can be used to treat cavities, but its possible side effects include gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and potential interactions with other medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a small dose, monitor your body's response, and avoid using Achillea millefolium teas for extended periods or in combination with other medications that may interact with its active compounds.
